description: An incremental model silently diverges from the truth because its late-arriving data window is too narrow.
body: Failure mode: an incremental model filters on rows newer than the last run, so backdated or updated source records arriving after the window are never picked up and the table drifts below a full refresh. Detection signals: a reconciliation diff between the incremental table and a full refresh growing over time, and totals trailing a trusted source. Do not just rerun the incremental build, which repeats the same gap. Safer fallback: widen the incremental predicate to a lookback window covering observed late arrivals, switch to a merge keyed on a unique id so updates are captured, full-refresh once to reset, and schedule ongoing reconciliation.
